commands.title: Check for vulnerable SUID binaries
commands.description: Lists files with the SUID bit set, which may be exploitable for privilege escalation.

commands.title: File & Directory Permissions
commands.template: find / -type f -perm -4000 2>/dev/null && find / -type f -perm -2000 2>/dev/null && find / -writable -type d 2>/dev/null && find / -readable -type f -exec ls -la {} \; 2>/dev/null
commands.description: Checks for SUID files, SGID files, writable directories, and readable files that may reveal sensitive information.

commands.title: Check sudo permissions
commands.description: Lists the commands the current user can run with elevated privileges.

commands.title: Check internal services
commands.description: Lists listening services and their associated ports.

commands.title: Check environment variables
commands.description: Prints environment variables which may contain sensitive information.

commands.title: Read .env file from web root
commands.description: Reads environment configuration file that may contain credentials or API keys.

commands.title: Check system mail
commands.description: Reads mailboxes for possible sensitive information.

commands.title: System information
commands.description: Displays detailed system and kernel version information.

commands.title: Shell profile files
commands.description: Reads shell initialization and logout scripts that may contain aliases, functions, or credentials.

commands.title: Search for backups
commands.description: Searches for backup files which may contain sensitive information.

commands.title: Check interesting folders
commands.description: Lists contents of directories that may contain backups, temporary files, or sensitive data.

commands.title: Root-owned writable files
commands.description: Finds files owned by root but writable by the current user.

commands.title: Root-owned readable files
commands.description: Finds files owned by root that are readable by the current user.

commands.title: Shell Configuration Files
commands.description: Common shell startup and configuration files for Bash and Zsh. These files control environment variables, aliases, and shell behavior. Login shells read `.bash_profile`, `.bash_login`, or `.profile` (in that order), while non-login shells read `.bashrc`. `/etc/profile` provides system-wide defaults. `.bash_logout` is read when a login shell exits. Zsh uses `.zlogin` and `.zshrc` for similar purposes.

commands.title: Run LinPEAS for automated enumeration
commands.description: Downloads and runs LinPEAS to automate privilege escalation checks.

commands.title: pspy64
commands.description: pspy is a Linux process monitoring tool that allows you to see commands run by other users, including root, without needing elevated privileges. It’s useful for detecting cron jobs, automated scripts, and other processes that could be exploited for privilege escalation.
